name: ml-antipattern-validator
description: Prevents 30+ critical AI/ML mistakes including data leakage, evaluation errors, training pitfalls, and deployment issues. Use when working with ML training, testing, model evaluation, or deployment.
capabilities: Detect 30+ ML antipatterns in Python code using AST analysis
Validate train/test splits for leakage patterns (temporal, group, sample overlap)
Check evaluation methodology correctness (CV strategies, metric alignment)
Review preprocessing order to prevent scaling leakage
Generate fix recommendations for detected issues
author: Doyajin174
securitySummary: This is a documentation and static analysis skill for ML antipattern detection. All static findings are FALSE POSITIVES triggered by code examples in documentation showing antipatterns that users should avoid. The Python scripts (scripts/detect_leakage.py, scripts/validate_split.py) only read local files using standard pandas for data validation. No network access, no credential access, and no command execution capabilities exist. Behavior matches stated purpose.
valueStatement: ML models often fail in production due to data leakage, improper evaluation, and training mistakes. This skill detects over 30 critical antipatterns and helps prevent invalid results by scanning your code and data splits before training.
limitations: Static analysis only - cannot catch runtime or logic errors
Requires Python files to be syntactically valid
Does not execute your ML code or access external APIs
Cannot verify statistical significance of detected issues

useCases.title: Validate training code
useCases.description: Scan training scripts for leakage before running experiments to ensure valid results

useCases.title: Review data splits
useCases.description: Validate train/test CSVs for group leakage, temporal ordering, and class distribution

useCases.title: Check evaluation methodology
useCases.description: Verify cross-validation setup and metric selection matches the business objective

promptTemplates.title: Quick scan
promptTemplates.prompt: @validate-ml scan my training script at src/train_model.py for data leakage and antipatterns

promptTemplates.title: Check data split
promptTemplates.prompt: @check-leakage validate my train.csv and test.csv files for group overlap using user_id column

promptTemplates.title: Review evaluation
promptTemplates.prompt: @verify-eval review my evaluation approach - I am using cross_val_score with 5 folds on time series data

promptTemplates.title: Full validation
promptTemplates.prompt: @validate-ml perform full validation on my ML pipeline - check preprocessing order, data splits, and evaluation setup

bestPractices: Always split data BEFORE any preprocessing (fit_transform, imputation, encoding)
Use Pipeline objects to ensure correct preprocessing order in cross-validation
Keep related samples (same user, patient, session) together in train/test splits
Never tune hyperparameters on test data - use a separate validation set
antiPatterns: Calling fit_transform on full dataset then splitting - leaks test statistics into training
Using train_test_split with shuffle=True on temporal data - breaks causality
Testing on training data or evaluating model.score(X_train, y_train)
Using accuracy on highly imbalanced datasets without checking per-class metrics

faq.question: Which ML frameworks are supported?
faq.answer: Works with any Python ML code using scikit-learn, PyTorch, TensorFlow, or custom implementations

faq.question: What file types can be scanned?
faq.answer: Python source files (.py) for code analysis and CSV files for data split validation

faq.question: Can this integrate with CI/CD pipelines?
faq.answer: The detect_leakage.py script can be run from command line and returns exit codes for CI integration

faq.question: Is my data sent anywhere?
faq.answer: No - all analysis runs locally. Files are read from disk but never transmitted externally

faq.question: What types of leakage are detected?
faq.answer: Target leakage, temporal leakage, preprocessing leakage, group leakage, and cross-validation fold leakage

faq.question: How does this compare to pytest or linters?
faq.answer: This tool focuses specifically on ML antipatterns that general linters cannot detect, such as data leakage patterns
